In other words, in a warm place he will freeze them all up one way or the other do something. I remember reading the thing, when Montezuma comes back, so I says when the snow falling it flashed right in my mind. Montezuma has returned, jokingly you know. 1:14 Came through Mexico, even then I didn’t want to teach meditation, he kept pressing, and then I came back from Mexico, visited the various ruins and came back and strange thing, but I went to visit SRF after you know and I met Sister Daya and they were going to India for the first time and she was very cold to me the first time I met her you know. She was strictly a typical secretary in charge of big organization and “who are you?” and anyway it was the funniest thing, she knew of me in one way and I asked her for her photograph, I talked to her, then I asked her for her blessing or darshan, but the strangest thing, Kriyananda was present in the room at that time and actually why I went up there was only because of a monk that was living there now was a student that I had started teach meditation way back in Canada had gone to live there and he was the person I really wanted to see and then she came in and then I got her blessing you know from her and Kriyananda was surprise, he said he’d never seen Sister do what she did. The whole room seemed like a vacuum and you could hear this vibration so strong going and the peace that flowed out and then we were leaving he says, “You know I’ve been here so long and I’ve been in Sister’s presence and I’ve never seen it.” The first time he saw something come through her and she was leaving for India. 1:16 Well when I left I said to Sonny, I says, “She is a very spiritual person but she hasn’t arrived at naturalness.” So Sonny says, “What do you mean?” I says, “Wait till she comes back from India then you’ll know what I mean.” And she bid me goodbye and she says, “I hope to see you when I come back.” Years passed and I’m coming back through that same area and even then I didn’t want to talk of meditation to nobody, coming through back that area and went to visit and I want to see my friend the monk and coming up the stairway there was a woman waiting to be interviewed and we came in the doorway to the receptionist and down that stairway, you know how Mount Washington is built, that stairway coming around the corridor, there she was coming down all dressed in Sari, her Indian clothes for the first time and we saw her but we didn’t make no sign to her and she came down and she greeted the person who was waiting for her, they went in and then we asked for the person we wanted to see, this monk, and they told us he’d be down, you wait in the library, so we went in the library and waited. All of a sudden the door opened and a head pushed through, peeping you know, and then she said “Hi,” one big hug you know, reunion and the energy come pouring down. Well you saw an entirely different person altogether, we spoke for about half an hour. In the meantime the other guy is stuck somewhere in the building, he’s not coming out, and we talk for awhile and everything and then she says, “Well I have to be going now and hope you come back.” And she left and walked out the door. 1:18 We were charged up so Sonny says, “Now I know what you mean by naturalness.” He says, “Yep, I got the picture.” I say “Yeah, she’s arrived.” Then we’re waiting and the door opened, the fellow came in and says, “Hi.” And we start talking and we says, “We just spoke to Sister Daya.” Well his face came long, “What?!” He says, “Man I’ve been living here so long and I’ve only seen Sister Daya twice in my whole life and you people right in. I didn’t even know she was back from India.” “Well you should’ve come down earlier.” He says, “You know when you called me on the phone and told me you were coming to see me.” I said, “Yes.” “You said you were going to postpone it until one o’clock and I told you no, come on now.” I said, “Yes.” He said, “Well I went upstairs to take a shower and I went down the corridor to get a shave and I cut my face and I had to go all the way down searching for a styptic you know” and he says, “Then I came down (inaudible) my little cut.” The other thing, he had been shaving so many times, that’s the first day he’s going to nick his face. I says, “Well your nicking your face was my good luck.” He says, “Look you people get to meet Sister Daya.” I says, “Well that’s the way it goes.” Anyway we went again back from there to Mexico. I didn’t want to meditate, I didn’t want to teach meditation, didn’t want to get involved with no group and we came back again to the States, we didn’t want to get involved, we were looking now for a place to spend time with ourselves to do 40 days and 40 nights fasting. 1:20 Even all that time this consciousness is going on, you know, I’m forcing myself not to get involved. I says, “I knew Yogananda wrote a book called the Whispers from Eternity and I want to find this place at 29 Palms. At that time I did not know it was in 29 Palms he wrote it, but I know he had a place. So we were searching all around to rent a house to live and meditate for awhile by ourselves in the high desert and we found this house, it was the strangest thing, it was the only house that was owned by some doctor and he gave it to real estate people to rent out and we rented the house for about $60 a month, everything, supplies and everything and we spend the whole month fasting and I would always look up to the mountains and figure, “Where can this Master’s house be that…” inquire, but somehow I never wanted to teach again meditation and Sonny kept asking me. When I came to Los Angeles afterwards and I ran into some people at the SRF church that started the whole train of events from then on, getting back to talk of meditation. Well they were all students of SRF, they lived so long in the area, but they did not know how to meditate and they attended all the classes and some of them were meditating. As soon as they came away from the place, you know, the Ashram or the church, and they had to work in the world, meditation just went out through the door. 1:22 I said, “I used to be an official temple leader,” but even then I gave that up so I’d have time to myself. I just wanted to meditate, that’s all, because this is the whole thing. (Inaudible) Well we got together the disciples, friendly, and meditate and that’s how it started the whole train of events till the last time I decided to quit again and in Utah now, it’s up in Utah when I had the vision of Jesus saying, “Well what are you doing, you stopped teaching meditation?” Well that’s the last time I says I can quit it, I have to come back and teach it. So I’ve been carrying it on from then I guess. Audience: Lucky for us. I’d hate to think what would happen without (inaudible). Adano: You see the thing is this, meditation… well it’s in the process of realizing itself. What time is it? Audience: About a quarter to three. Adano: You’ve got the bardo book? You see I was familiar with Gurdjieff’s work… when I read Ouspensky, it was the funniest thing, I read Ouspensky after and I read Ouspensky’s work… I thought you had the one by Leary who had it all laid out, Timothy Leary’s psychedelic description of the bardos? This here is alright but you have to dig through all the… Audience and Adano: Several inaudible conversations regarding the bardo book. 1:31 You see the thing is don’t for one moment… what you are seeing on the inner planes, don’t think you… on the inner planes most people want to think they gotta be ultra beautiful more than what you look here. You see the man is seeing an electrical field. If it is an electrical field, the consciousness is gonna be there, but I have to check that out. I have to see him personally to really say that is or that is not what is happening. He might have seen something and he’s trying to bring it to that drawing. Audience: Or what’s the alternative? Adano: I have to see him first to (inaudible). This is what it is you know, the deeper levels you go in, the more and more this consciousness comes down. That is what the meditative life, the same way you progressing outwardly, you are progressing inwardly. The outward life is also part of the inward life. So the deeper you go into meditation, the deeper the bliss gets and it is supposed to open up… Audience: I haven’t been sleeping, that’s for sure. (More miscellaneous and inaudible conversations.) 1:40 (Adano blesses a St. Christopher’s medal) Saint Christopher is one of my favorite saints you know, he saved my life a couple of times. Two days ago he did. I’m coming along with the truck, it was foggy two days ago in the morning and all the cars are stopped to permit me to pass and it’s foggy so I go into first gear from stop to first and I’m just starting to move across the car over the section and a girl came at the speed of 60 miles right in front of me and I stopped just dead and her car didn’t stop and I’m calm and I saw her go down the road and everybody is looking. She’d have been dead. Her face where the front of the truck was, where I was coming, her body was exactly in line dead center of vision. In the fog, because everybody stopped she kept on inside right and shot right across and they had all stopped to give me the right of way and I just start moving off to go across from the opposite side of the street and vroom, I put the brake on in time and I can just see the handle of the door, about that much difference. You know I says, “Well thanks, she got lucky this morning” and I’m lucky this morning too because I know if I hit her smacked in, both of us would be pretty… she would have been killed. Audience: But how were you helped by this Saint? Adano: Well when I drive, I really don’t drive for myself, I always pray to Saint Christopher because he’s the traveler, of motion, he’s the saint who moves in motion. 1:42 You see Christopher is the traveler’s Saint and he’s supposed to have walked across the water and carried the baby infant Jesus on his shoulder and he has been blessed as the Saint of the travelers. You always see him with a big stick and he’s walking across water, a river that he wanted to cross and he’s carrying this baby across. He’s supposed to be given the blessing to aid travelers that’s why they called him St. Christopher, the traveler’s aid or the traveler’s Saint so I have a St. Christopher on me. You see when I drive I don’t only turn to the Masters, I turn to all the Saints, each one has their own particular purpose. Audience: Where is this Saint right now? Adano: St. Christopher has been dead thousands of years ago. Audience: Well we believe in reincarnation so where is he? Adano: He’s on the inner plane, he’s a realized man. Well you see apart from that the Masters are protecting too you know, but when I get in the car I always say, “Well St. Christopher, you’re driving. You are the driver of the car.” Audience: I’ll do that next time (laughter), see what happens (more laughter). Adano: Well I trust myself too you know. It’s better to have a little extra trust from all around you know. 1:45 Audience: Where do you draw the line? Maybe it’s a naive question and everybody else knows the answer. Where do you draw the line… I have certain responsibilities, I have come here and been assigned a task which I’m supposed to fulfill. This is one thing and then we say that we submit ourselves to Divine Mother, let Her will be, but a person who is not so developed like you are, a person at a different level can use this as a pretext and sort of fail at his responsibility and this is wrong, you are supposed to do your duty, assigned task. Now where do you draw the line? I don’t know, this does bother me at times and maybe I think there are people at a higher level who got bothered like Arjuna, you know “What is my duty and what is not.” Where am I supposed to do something and take the responsibility? And if I fail I put myself to task and I say Divine Mother is going to do it or take care of it. Adano: Well let me put it this way, Divine Mother is not doing anything for you, you’re doing everything for the Divine Mother, but you have to tell yourself that She’s doing it for you otherwise you wouldn’t do it for Her. Audience: What’s that, I didn’t understand. Adano: You see if you want somebody to do something for you is to tell them not to do it (laughter). Alright then you see that’s what Divine Mother… Ok we realize this so quick, huh. Audience: No what I mean is Adano that this is what has happened in certain instances that under this thing that yes (inaudible) when we are really not at higher level under this pretext sometimes what you call apathy or not doing what you’re role is to do and then you are committing the sin of neglecting your duty… Adano: Wait, this is a fine point in which the Master (inaudible). We are all performing action from a past condition. 1:48 In other words what you are doing right now was already preset to be completed in this (inaudible). See now this karmic action is occurring right now cannot be cashed in right now, immediately, because it has been preset to occur now, you see? So you are living out something that is preset to occur now. Now you cannot evaluate what is happening right now, you have to wait for a month or two months or three months to evaluate what has just happened, you see? So this is a condition that is happening. When you say you are doing your duty and where does the fine line drawn between God doing it for you or you’re doing God’s will or you’re doing your own will or your own responsibility, is the evaluation three months from now when you evaluate over four years of experiences and the odd, as you would say, or the unusual way how these experiences fit themselves and came out in the conditions they came out. You begin to see now from a deeper insight that it is the will of God in motion, but in the meantime while you are involved in it, you may not see that, you may see your personal involvement, your personal need to exert your own will upon it or your own control upon it. So the fine line is only based on evaluation. That’s why I say, Divine Mother is really not doing anything for us, we’re doing everything for Her, you see? 1:50 But in a sense it’s only relative, what is really happening again is this, the will of God is that karma must be completed, this is the will of God because He cannot alter the karma, you see? “As you sow that you reap,” this is the principle. Now if we think in specific conditions and if we think in the specific involvement of the conditions without understanding that these are only conditions for our behavior, then we automatically suffer guilt in ourselves as not performing in a real way and not doing the utmost and we think we are shirking our duties, but this is not true. You are performing under the best possible conditions afforded you, with the best possible abilities you have available, with the most practical understanding you have at the time. You couldn’t have done it any better or any worse. Audience: But supposed I know better than say I do… Adano: Knowing from within that we can do better and that we should do better has nothing to do with the condition in which you are involved at the moment. Now the reason why we know we should do better and we ought to do better is because more conditions will be afforded for us to demonstrate this creative nature of doing better. Every year we have a new model of the car, the model of the car has not reached perfection because life is continuity and since consciousness is continuity we feel this infinite pull of inadequacy and this is what we call “the new model in consciousness,” the rebirth of consciousness from moment to moment. 1:52 It’s not the rebirth of body that is more important, it’s a reconstituting of consciousness from moment to moment of demonstrating the refinement process. So if I feel 10 years ago or 5 years ago conditions that I was involved in and the way I did it and I look back, “I could have done better,” I realize that I couldn’t have done any more or any less, but that was exactly what was supposed to have been done because that’s the shape of the piece of the jigsaw puzzle and that was the only way it was gonna fit, you can’t fit it any more or less. So it is no tearing down of the mechanism because this is strictly gonna be a sort of a self punishment to constantly tear down the shape of the structure to try to fit it into another shape where it was supposed to be fitted in the first place. If you take a jigsaw puzzle and you got all the pieces cut up in front of you and you start fitting it, you got some people who put them together by shaving the edges to fit it, never allowing for the jigsaw puzzle piece that was the piece that gotta fit there, you know? So some people I’ve seen them play, they come out with a jigsaw puzzle, shaved it to fit (laughter). The perfectionist shaving the thing to fit in and it don’t really belong there, you see, but (inaudible) because the parts are already preset so. It is a reconciliation with ourcells that, in order to maintain the humility in the mechanism, in order to maintain the grace of God flowing to us, is to accept that what we are doing is being done with the best of our abilities under the conditions we are doing it. 1:54 Now to say we have to do better than that, this is true only because more conditions will be forthcoming to allow us to do it better, you understand? Now you can do something good, but you can do something better, and you can do something at the best, but unless you have these different grades or these three conditions to allow you evaluate the three grades, you don’t know which one is good, which one is best, and which one is better because you would have no way to compare. So supposed you do something now and in your mind you think you have done the best at that particular moment you are involved in it and four years from now you say, “Gee whiz I did the worst.” Because your sense of appreciation, your sense of evaluation, your sense of involvement has grown past that limitation, past that point of understanding to something more now, to more involvement, you start to say, “Well I could have done better.” Let’s say in 1943 you did this particular thing a certain way and now this is 1959, this is the way you would do it and you look back, “I could have done this at that time in ‘43, better.” You start to see all the things you could have done better when it was already finished and done. 1:56 Audience: Yeah but not really. I see when I look back as you are talking about evaluation, that you really are able to evaluate later on you can exactly see where this is the kind of experience I needed and how it has helped me to grow further and you can see the meaning which didn’t make any meaning to you at that time you were going through all this, “what is this?” Adano: Quite true, this is the thing that is conflict with where the will fits in, where is your responsibility, where is Divine responsibility? It can’t be assessed in the present moment. It is the Divine Will in the present moment and all the time it is the Divine Will in the present moment and at a future time then you’re gonna start to decipher or separate which part of the Divine Moment is really Divine Moment and which part you think you took an active part in it. Then you realize the Guru says, “No, God was doing it through you in the first place as inadequate as you think it was inadequate and as perfect as you think it was perfect.” Because that’s how God works, who is doing the whole thing in the first place. Who’s He entertaining? Is He entertaining perfection or is He entertaining the interplay of perfection? There’s a big difference to say “God is entertaining Himself through Man as perfection,” than to say “God is entertaining Himself through Man as an interplay of perfection.” An interplay of perfection would mean there are levels of evaluating perfection, but to interplay, to work through Man as perfection, that means there is no room for any evaluation no more. If you are perfect, you don’t know what’s imperfect already, and if you’re imperfect you don’t know what’s perfect, you see? So if God is gonna to act through you as perfect, this is restricted, this is limitation. If God is going to act through you as imperfect, this also is the limitation, but if God is gonna act through as an interplay between perfection and imperfection, this is the freedom, this is where the evaluation comes, this is where you observe now why there is no limitation, this is all infinite now, it’s all a flux, it’s all a flow. The Maharishi would say, “We live in a flux between the relative and the absolute,” The relative and the absolute, no start no end, so don’t put a limitation that it’s imperfect, don’t put a limitation that it’s perfect either, because perfection and imperfection are all dualities, you see? And God’s Will and your will is still duality. 1:58 Audience: Yeah if the person can probably eliminate the ego and identify themselves with… Adano: Right, now why the ego comes in you see it’s because of individuality. We have been already trained to stand on our own two feet. Now many of us are not standing on our own two feet, we are standing on our heads (laughter) or we are all sitting down or something or we’re all kneeling down, the thing is, the whole thing is Divine Will. When I say Divine Will I don’t mean that there is some individual poking us with a pitchfork, moving us forward, no I don’t mean that. Divine Will is like a flow that… you see if you have a pipe here and you have water it has to flow from one point to another point. Now so Divine Will moves everything from negative to positive, it doesn’t move everything from positive to negative, it down’t work that way because the negative had to be made first before the positive can come into existence. In other words, He had to say to the first two people in the Garden of Eden, “Don’t do these things” which is negative, in order for them to do it, in order to come to the positive. Audience: He had to have an adversary. Adano: Right, He had to make them move from a negative condition into a positive condition so right away they were going to apply themselves to a negative principle, which is not a bad principle because there’s no such thing as a bad principle, it is a principle how motion begins. 2:00 The laws of motion, apart from it being spiritual or non-spiritual or mechanical, the law of motion itself is setup by the Divine Intelligence to move from negative to positive, to bring home the devotee from relativity to absolute, you understand? So Man is already positive in his nature but he doesn’t know how to move from positive to positive. If you are positive in nature how can you move from positive to positive, you don’t know where the next positive is, you see? Now we say in magnetism “unlike things attract each other and like things repel each other,” this is magnetism you see, this is the law of magnetism, positive attracts negative and positive repels positive in the actual laws of magnetism. If you have this object here and we take this object and this is a positive end and this is a negative end and let’s say this is a positive end and this is a negative end, the moment you bring these two positives they’ll be pushing each other apart. Now for them to come together you have to let the negative and the positive come, but in the human being it’s reversed. You see in the human being it’s different, positive attracts positive, positive and negative repel each other. Only in the human being is the law in reverse, it’s not in matter itself you see? So negatively we say we are drawn to people of like mind, of like feeling, in order to be polarized. Now people with unlike minds and unlike feelings depolarize us, demagnetize us. 2:02 So in the Divine Will everything is in this Divine Will to move from negative to positive so because it’s moving from negative to positive, it’s a fine line of where my responsibility lie, but because you are already thinking in a positive way and trying to take the bull by the horn and run this world by our limited way of thinking, we think that what we are doing is insufficient, we’re not doing enough to impress… who in the first place are we doing to impress? You ask yourself, who are we really trying to impress when we take the bull by the horn or take this world and try to run this thing? We have an obligation to do, but the obligation is strictly involved in behavior. The thing itself, the action itself is universal. Now here’s some children and you are the parent, you are liable for these children. Why are you liable? At a certain society, in a certain culture, for a certain age, the liability ends or begins or continues. Now at a certain level it’s not a liability because in an Ashram everything is a liability, everything is a responsibility. In an individual’s house, not everything is a liability, you’re not liable for your neighbor’s child, you’re not responsible for your neighbor’s children unless they hire you as a, what you do call it?… child watcher… Audience: Babysitter? Adano: Babysitter, alright. Then you are liable, then you are responsible to that degree, but as far as your neighbor… 2:04 You wouldn’t want to see your neighbor’s child run out and get squashed by a car so there is concern, not liability, it’s concern. So you’re concerned to see that the child stays within the framework of safety, but in a Guru it’s a different story. He is already decided to volunteer to carry out the Divine Will of helping the individual to move back from negative to positive. He is already like the garage mechanic who comes with a truck to help you with your stalled car to bridge the two batteries together to hook’em up to give you a boost. He’s already decided to come along and do it for you, you know? You’re stalled in the way you need the help, you’re waving your hand and here He comes along and bridges you. He is taking a different responsibility, He volunteered that responsibility. Now He couldn’t volunteer that responsibility unless He had mastered the laws of behavior, you see? If He didn’t master these laws of behavior He couldn’t volunteer to it so there is no taking away from Him. His actions have no tearing down, His actions strictly necessary for the continuity of life, His actions are what you call the Sattvic actions. We have the three Gunas, the three modes of action, the three modes of feeling, the Tamasic, Rajasic, and Sattvic. 2:06 Now Tamasic as far as food is concerned is yesterday’s food eat today, already being cooked and eaten today, like cake you know, that’s Tamasic. Now Rajasic is highly spiced food cooked now, eaten all up, get belly ache. Sattvic food is pick it off the vine, eat it, use it when you need it. Now that is in relation to… in mode of action Tamasic would be total indifference, they don’t care if the place is falling down, they don’t make an effort to do anything. Now Rajasic is “well if I do anything, it’s only for myself” and it’s all self centered and Sattvic “well I can’t do it for myself and I can’t do without not doing it, then I have to it for the betterment of the environment,” you see? So it’s necessary for the continuity of my existence so that is a Sattvic action, it is something that you’re gonna do for the continuity of existence that is around you the environment so this is now we call it in a nice terminology, humanitarian, very humanistic, very charitable actions, very spiritual, but it’s spiritual already from the start you volunteering to do something. Now also it can be very egotistical by believing to yourself that you can change and busy find ways to change the environment, to improve upon the environment, when you are not improving upon yourself, you see? When you should be improving upon your inner self, the environment is being changed and you’re not improving. This again, like Kriyananda says, many of us go out as reformers trying to change society when we really need to change in our own inner consciousness how to be good to ourselves and to our neighbor so this has a problem. 2:08 Now Divine Will again is like the ground, ego will or personal will is like a seed, now if you plant tomatoes you’re bound to get corn. So if we perform wrong actions we’re bound to get good karma (laughter), right? Since it don’t work that way that we know of, ego ain’t gonna to bring us anything more than we plant in the thing. If we plant tomatoes, we gotta get tomatoes, if we plant more ego then we get more ego. Divine Will is to make sure that you reap your ego. It makes sure you reap your tomato when you plant your tomato, this is the Divine Will. In other words, you get what you sow in the flow of things. Now to say we are doing Divine Will, we really don’t do Divine Will, we fit ourselves into the Divine Will. We don’t make the tomato grow, we don’t make a thought come into manifestation, a thought comes into manifestation because it is the flow of how thoughts must come into existence, how the action must follow-up, how people respond or react to the thought. We don’t make this happen, it’s happening for us, you see? So Jesus says, “Not my will but Thy will be done.” You alone bring into manifestation what is the practical or the necessary conditions for this mechanism to pass through. 2:10 This mechanism is now sacrificed or… when I say sacrificed I don’t mean thrown away, it’s being offered up voluntary now to take on the challenge as a seed going in the ground to die and to be germinated and grow up. Now He has to die on a cross to grow up into spiritual manhood, into a new tree, a resurrected tree now, you see? So it is a Divine Will that He voluntarily plant the seed of His body into the truth and reap the thing instead of exerting His own ego against it and not trying to go along. Now we can do this, we don’t put the seed in the ground to make it sprout, we exert our ego by taking a bucket full of water we throw the seed inside and let it sprout in the water then when it’s sprouted we can take it out and put it in the ground and let it root in the ground. Otherwise if you’re throwing it into the ground, the ground itself will take a little longer to sprout it, you see, but we just speed up the sprouting by putting it in water. So we took a hand now, we feel we’re that smart, you learned that much now about (inaudible) so we can sprout the thing and cause it to come out quicker. Coming about into sprouting quicker don’t alter the fact that this condition of dying and being born again has to occur, you see? If it die in the water by sprouting it or dying in the ground and sprouting it, the time ratio between the two is an ego condition. The time ratio between me doing the thing and God doing the thing is still a time condition. When I do it, it’s gotta be done right now, now, now, now, when God do it, maybe tomorrow, maybe next week, but it’s not important when God does it or when I do it, it is done. 2:12 See I’m in Gretna and I say, “Ok I’d like to come up here.” My ego would drive me (inaudible) fast, you got to get up here, throw an invisible lasso and pull him in, or throw the thing and pull him in. This is ego but if Divine Will is moving, the thought is already planted in consciousness, and to show you why it must be Divine Will when it manifests that every intricate piece of the conditions all fall into place because of the subtle nature, the subtle feelings of the people involved who are going to be affected by this thing when it falls into place, that that thing occurred. Now like this afternoon when Helen was on the phone, see, if all these things are not taken into consideration for each individual, look how many people are automatically reacting, you have to have consideration… So Divine Will is already taking into consideration all the different repercussions all down the line before one little act comes into manifestation that they say, “Well gee whiz, Divine Mother is so good to me.” You think of how many times Divine Mother has mathematically satisfied everybody with all these things? So you are in the (inaudible). So to say “I’m not doing my duty and my responsibility” is a very good question. Should I sit down and listen to Adano talk and forget my home life, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week? This sounds kind of ridiculous after. The guru would say “Go home and meditate.” I don’t think in the ashram that you sit down at the Master’s feet all day and talk to Him, you see? 2:14 The same thing is They make you conscious of a relationship with God and yourself and a consciousness of your obligation to yourself and the duties around you but it’s all in balance. Now the ashrams or the satsangas are primarily seminars in this behavior of yourself and God and it is a relationship with a daily organized way of life. If we live in the ashram then food and clothing and housing and some type of physical, manual service is being provided for. So you can pursue the meditative life without living with the fear of the lack of these things, that is the shelter, the clothing, you can’t wear more than one suit (inaudible), you can’t eat more than one meal (inaudible), but these things are not lacking, it’s no “Mother Hubbard in her cupboard.” You know Mother Hubbard went to her cupboard and she found it all bare and came back and had a bone (laughter). Now you see the thing is this is because in our consciousness if we don’t have these things we start to think, “Well we got bills to pay (inaudible). We can’t meditate.” Audience: I was just going to say, what about bills? (Laughter) Adano: Well Sister Daya can answer that pretty well, she said “Master we have bills to pay, when are we going to pay it, we need some money” and you know Master says, “Sister, you’ve done exceedingly well with what you’ve got, continue as you are.” 2:16 So Sister walks away and the bill is not paid but before you know it something comes along, they put on some extra lecture, something to raise funds and the bills are paid. Now in our individual lives it’s the same thing. Sri Yukteswar says, “Live comfortable within your earnings.” In other words don’t be too extravagant because the earning power is in relation to the spending power, but then there’s a seed power. You can’t plant without seed and this is the origin of the insurance, the insurance people were smart when they figure it out. If you can’t plant without seed you gotta put away some and if you put away some, you’re gonna to pull it out before you use it, so why not sell the people insurance against themselves? So you insure the people. Now one yogi says, “The greatest insurance policy is God” so he says, “I’m not building up the insurance houses, I’m building up God in myself, this is my insurance policy” but then another yogi comes along and says, “That’s all good and fine, but if you’ve got an organization and you don’t want it to burn down so you might as well insure it so it is taken care of.” So use every law, use every principle, use the principle of divine insurance by meditation and use the principle of physical insurance by taking care of the thing if these calamities occur. Divine insurance is the forestall it happening, physical insurance is to reimburse you if it did happen. 2:18 And did you see that picture, “Lillies of the Valley” what did the Mexican man said he was doing when they were building the church? He didn’t believe there was a God, but he was busy giving merchandise and service and what is it he said, “He’s buying insurance.” Audience: Just in case there is a God (laughter). Adano: Story about Bernstein’s tribute upon Kennedy’s death called Kaddish.

Adano68_11_25BauereisQ2RRNW6 - (Meditation) The mind has a tendency to drift and there are waves that pass through the mind and bring certain ideas, we find our attention seems to latch onto these ideas and get stuck on the ideas. Ideas from the past generate frustration in the mind. Ideas from the future generate anxiety around that idea. If the mind would stay in the present now, “Meekly wait and murmur not” in self-observation, then the mind would experience peace because the present is peace. It’s the gap between frustration and anxiety, between past and future, it is the eternal Now where you actually do live. It is the only reality. Now is the process of participation, you are in the presence all the time. In this nowness is expansion, it is an internal integration of the three forces in your mechanism: physical, mental, and spiritual. You are Soul and the connection between the Soul to a frame called the body lies in the breath: incoming and outgoing breath. This incoming / outgoing function is one of love, devotion, conscious recognition / identity / oneness. To stray from this point of reference from within one’s self is to go away from the source of peace and tranquility into the stream of agitation and tension. To withdraw from tension is to release the reservoir of peace that passes all understanding. Open the eyes partially, not completely. To speed it up we will close the eyes completely and dive deeper into the source. 0:10 (Meditation continues) If we are desiring internal peace then there is no need for internal will, there will be an internal suspension of our own will permeating our entire body. This suspension is the Divine Will, we have become synchronized with the Divine Will, we have become united with the Divine Will. Our feeling of oneness is intensified. We share this oneness with everyone in the room from within hence in reality we are truly loving our neighbors as ourselves and we are loving the creative principle, God, with all our mind, heart, and soul. And the internal experience traveling up the spinal column, like a tingling cool/warm sensation, is the abundant life that the Master Jesus said, “The I AM hath come that we may have life more abundantly.” The I AM begins the release the flow of life in us, transmuting our cells from the coarse expression to a finer, subtle expression with a great deal of internal delicacy, tenderness, and compassion. Because we are creatures of habit, the mechanism internally is craving activity of some sort. In the present process, the highest activity is to partially open the eyes and feel your nature, your existence, your oneness, your universal nature / oneness. To gradually close the eyes completely and go back into the source, peace. This is truly being active while inactive from within to the surface of the mechanism. Go in and out as many times as you need to to experience the flow of the inner source to the surface of the mechanism. 0:20 (Meditation continues) As we become more sensitive and aware of the peace within ourselves and is receptive to it and to the luminescence present in the skull or the radiant light and the bliss permeating the body, we discover as we gradually open our eyes, an inner strength coming to the surface. A regenerative condition taking over the entire body, a recharging of the mechanism, a rejuvenating of the mechanism. No internal craving to burn up energy unnecessarily, but to reabsorb that which is dormant within us and allow it to flow freely for the first time abundantly. Slowly open the eyes completely but don’t move, observing the tranquility of the mechanism, observing the non-drifting of the mind from the present, holding on to this feeling in the waking state into the active state. How long it remains with you, that is the measurement of our spirituality. This feeling in the active state is highly magnetic and highly therapeutic. This state in the active state is an internal immunization against tension in the environment but you have to hold onto it. Anytime you find the environment will disrupt this state, you can always close those eyes and go back to the source, remembering Master Jesus saying “Come unto Me all that travail and are heavily laden and I will refresh you.” In reality, He was referring to the involvement of the senses in the environment of the human being. The sensory nature becomes laden down with a crosscurrent of activity and there’s traffic jams in the nervous system, conflict again between anxiety and frustration, and the whole cycle starts again, but we have a way in to the source, we have a way into the immunization process, we have in to the therapeutic process, and we have a way to maintain it in the active state daily, moment-to-moment in the eternal now. Let us dive one more time very deeply within ourselves with the deepest love possible that we can generate for the source, for we are the source, Jesus said, “I and the Father are one.” The source and you are the same, two things equal to the same thing are themselves equal to one another, this is a reality, this is peace that passes all understanding. This source is portable, it can be taken with you anyplace because it is within you. The method of application is universal, a shift in the movement of the eyes, the experience is common to all and also universal. The fruits of the action and the process are peace, extra sensitivity or awareness, strength to face our responsibilities in the active world without being destroyed by the frustration or the anticipation or the tension. 0:30 Slowly open the eyes. Thank you. (End of meditation) Meditation cannot be read out to get results, it has to be experimented with, it has to be applied moment to moment, that’s why Master Jesus said, “When two or three are gathered in My name, there am I in their midst.” This is active participation of people, it’s difficult by yourself alone, but with a few other people there’s a balancing out, there’s a synchronizing, there’s an interplay, an exchange of the various qualities, feelings, and the various levels of awareness in each individual. It has never been taught in the churches because in the first place, it takes a long time to reevaluate ourselves when we live in ideas concerning past and future and when our emotional nature is trapped between frustration and anxiety to widen the gap between past and future in order to live in the present where there is the only reality existing. Two oppositional states or fluctuations of the mind, but we need them very badly sometimes they are so important to the growth, this fluctuation, it reduces our ego and makes us humble. God has waited for us all billions of years to sit down and meditate, you’ve been sitting here the past couple of hours waiting for Adano to arrive, can you imagine the interplay of tension, anxiety, frustration that’s going on in the mechanism? We need that interplay to reevaluate between what is humility, what is tranquility, what is ego, what is tension, if we don’t have the opposition, we do not know if our spiritual growth is a strong one or a weak one, then we will live in a false concept that we are very spiritual. In fact we are not until we are tested so in the spiritual or meditative life, which is the moment to moment realization of ourselves, trial, the opposition, the conditions that challenge us, are all the prerequisites for grading the behaviour of this mechanism. The word Satan in Hebrew means adversary, opposition, Maya, duality, relativity, these are only names for the intrinsic resistance in nature to test the internal potential of the consciousness. It’s the intrinsic resistance of nature to test the internal potential of consciousness, God. He cannot destroy His opposition, He only used personalities because we are taking Judeo-Christian concepts or Eastern concepts and personalize them because we see ourselves as form, in actuality there is no form, consciousness has no form, energy has no form, it’s all bliss, it’s all light, but without the form there is no devotion, there is no compassion, there is no tenderness. How can you be endearing to a wife that has no form? We must have form, this is why two are necessary, the opposition, the importance of it. We are trying in meditation to establish within a mechanism that has been classified by the scriptures as a temple for the living God, but we need tangible experiments, tangible experience to validate it and the only way we can have this tangible experiment, this tangible experience, is to get involved with it and the they used to say “skin don’t you know me?” I’ll tell you a little story of “skin, don’t you know me,” one time a missionary was traveling through the reservation and it was in the middle of winter and he saw the indian Chief with only his blanket around his body, standing up and looking down the mountain, and the missionary was wrapped up and he said to him “Chief, how come you don’t feel cold in old a blanket” and the Chief looked at him and said, “Tell me priest, do you cover your face in the wintertime?” He says, “No.” Then he pulled his blanket apart, “Me all face.” 0:40 His philosophy was simple and direct to the point because when we get to ourselves, we cannot escape our bodies, only time we have a temporary relief daily is when we’re sound asleep and don’t know who we are and when we wake up, “skin, don’t you know me?” Is the same thing, you got to live it all over again, but it’s important because this is the instrument to work with. It is a marvelous instrument, I don’t think any other form in creation could serve the purpose for God realization. God made Man, or the man species, in His own image, atomic, and in His own likeness, Universal mindedness or total memory, male and female created them, no inequality, we’re all equal, and breathed into this man species the breath of life, and this man species became living soul, you don’t have soul. When we live with the concept that we have a soul, we are searching all over the universe for ourselves. When we realize we are soul, we wake up with this inner consciousness working for the first time putting us at the right place, at the right time, for the right experience, and right experience don’t mean good or bad, right experience necessary for evolution, necessary for self-evaluation, necessary for service, necessary for the continuity of life as a whole, no limitation. That’s why Jesus could not be the only son of God, in His own words He proves it, “The things I do you shall do also and lesser things shall you do.” Not like Me, you can never reach up to My estate, did He say that? He said “And greater things shall you do” proving beyond all doubt, refuting all types of propaganda, that He IS the son of God. The Christ Intelligence is the son of God, but not s-o-n, it’s the radiant sun within this mechanism because this body is light already, it’s a radiant sun we perceive, it’s the intuitive self, this is the Christ intelligence. The Master Jesus was a physical form that attained it and Chrystos is a Greek word meaning “attained” and Kutastha Chaitanya is the Sanskrit word for Christ consciousness or Krishna, Gopal the Krishna, one who has attained. So and only by religious drawings or paintings can we comprehend why it is an attainment and not a person. In Jewish writings, since they were not a nation of sculptors and artisans, they never portrayed their depth of realization in art, but in writing. Other oriental nations portrayed the depth of their realization in art and color, so the Krishna, or the Christ, self is painted in blue (Adano laughs) and when you meditate, there is a blue flame burning within your forehead and within it the radiant forms of all the great Sages pass before you in that radiant light. Now as it is in Heaven so it is on Earth, we are a miniature universe, the outward universe has the same colors, the golden beam that encircles the surface between the earth and the sky where it meets at dawn or sunset, the Holy Spirit or Holy Vibration, the golden ring is right around us here. In the center of that golden ring is blue, opalescent blue, the whole cosmos is blue and within that blue is a silver like star or cosmic star, five pointed, pointing downward like a dove in profile. Now anyone passing inwards to that inner radiation or inner light would see the same thing, “And this is my beloved Son in whom I am well pleased.” If the I-AM principle could not be pleased with Its cosmic light stamped within the brain of the man species indicating that the intuitive self is the highest principle in the man species, what other principle would it want to love or would it want the form of the five senses to love, you see? Now Jesus attained this, Jesus had a total awareness of this so uniquely balanced, that’s why He said to His fellow man, “The things I do, you’ll do also, and greater things you shall do.” “And he that doeth the will of the Father is My brother and My sister,” no inequality “And he that followeth in My word is my disciple.” Now disciples always argue, brothers and sisters in the spirit never argue, two Masters of two different religious cultures meeting, the greatest greeting is “Peace, Love.” Two disciples of two different Masters meeting, “Well my Master said this the other day.” “Well my Master said this the other day.” “Well let’s find out what your Master really means.” This is what destroys all of us, we don’t have no love, we start comparing what the Master said and what the Master really means. It’s what the disciple thinks the Master really said or what the disciple thinks about what the Master said is the problem, but the meditation is the leveling agent for all of us. It will betray the ridiculousness of the disciple and it will expose the reality of the Master. So the Master is not afraid to be expose so He says, “Look inside.” All Masters will tell you, this is the only path, don’t be afraid to be exposed because it’s you and Me the same thing, disciples “Oh no, my technique has to be better than yours. My way of doing it has got to be better than yours.” Afraid to be exposed because have they really experienced it? They may have something going for them, but if this something is really genuine we don’t know until they are willing to expose themselves. Meditation bridges the gap and the fruit of the meditation from the Master’s standpoint is one, it’s common, it’s universal. From the disciple’s standpoint, it’s contradiction. The Master’s from their standpoint says the fruit of meditation is peace, not that the world can give you by drugs, the peace I give, the world can’t give you. Life abundant, sensitivity, what do they want of this physical frame that they cannot give the dead man, They have the power of life over death, strength. Carry your own cross, don’t carry mine. The disciple, he doesn’t see that, he sees conflict, contradiction, he doesn’t see peace. 0:50 He sees the miracles, the phenomena around the life of the Master and the first time when the Master’s life is threatened in any culture, he’s the first one to deny knowing the Master. “I don’t know you, never heard of you” and you’ll find that’s true of all of us including myself. This is part of our nature, why? Because it’s a process of being involved with the fact or the concept that you have a soul or you are soul. If you have a soul you’re going to deny the existence of a Master once His life is threatened because in fact the Master is soul, He’s a living example of soul, and He is aggravating you with all your false beliefs that you have a soul or that you don’t know you are soul and if you really don’t know you are soul, you’re going to deny because he’s threatening your very social structure. All the things you’ve built your life around, all the language structure, all the social customs, this is threatening it. So the meditation again comes as a leveling agent. When the Master’s life is taken away by the rot of the individuals in the environment, then the disciple is despondent, he’s despair, “I have failed, I’m no good.” All kind of self-guilt comes in but when that happens we start seeking solitude, to console himself, it’s in that moment therapy begins. In that moment, meditation for the first time is occurring, all the other time he was dabbling in it, no knowing where he’s going. Now for the first time, confirmation of values, understanding the real purpose of a Master’s life. He then begins to see himself truly, it’s not His body, it’s the principle. Master truly was the principle of divinity, “Where is my principles. Where am I gathering, where am I going to the source?” Is the source inside? Ok, solitude, you know like a yogi, sit like a lump for the next 25 years to get the principle. It’s very true, but we don’t have to sit like a lump to get the principle, the principle is here, the next one. If we don’t have the next one, the principle ends because the scriptures say, “God breathed into Man the breath of life and Man became a living Soul.” So if the principle is already there, the practice is here. Now not to hold it in or hold it out, that’s not the principle, the principle is that we are hooked into this process by an incoming / outgoing flow and the incoming / outgoing flow separates us between past and future, between anxiety and frustration, we stay in the center which is the present, which is the now. No holding in or holding out of breath, it’s a realization between the two states of consciousness afforded by breath to a mechanism that has to breathe to realize it. Now if this breath was not afforded to us as our daily bread, we’d be long gone, and as one person said, “Why didn’t He give us jam and butter too? Why didn’t He give us a little more comprehension in this process when it was being bestowed upon us?” He gave us the comprehension, but like a seed, it can’t sprout until you do something to it. You can’t eat the fruit of the seed until you germinate it and let it grow like a tree and then reap the fruit. The comprehension cannot come out of the mechanism unless we water it by effort. This process of meditation is the same thing. You realize why mediation is something you do and can’t talk about. Now you should not feel offended if someone say you’re wasting your time, they’re entitled to it. The question is in the long run when you have achieved your peace of mind and he is still in his nervous tension and your body is in the process of regenerating and revitalizing and gracefully aging and his is deteriorating and rapidly aging, you have the testimony before your own eyes. Who has been wasting his time? There is God, one good fact that meditation affords us this realization. It’s the source that nourishes, it’s the source that let’s the consciousness flow out. It’s the same source that permits you to feel. If this source is common to all of us then we realize why we can dip into it. A good example is like the current in the walls, there’s a 110 to 120 volts coming into this building, that’s a common voltage, that is like the vital power that pours into us by the medulla oblongata or the mouth of God in Man and you plug in and light up one bulb, you plug in and light up your toaster, you plug in and light up your radio, these are all different areas or conditions to plug into the source. Now you don’t have one type of bulb with one type of wattage in the entire building, you have 100 watt, 80 watt, 20 watt, 200 watt for different purposes. Wattage in Man is receptivity, degree of karma, degree or capacity to cope, but in Man it is not like the bulb that is fixed, Man has a variable capacity because Man is tissue, elastic in nature. That’s the uniqueness of Man species, Man has the vital current flowing into him just like the ant and Man has the same source and common bond with the ant. Now if the Buddha says “all creatures are our brothers and sisters,” it’s only from that common source of the flow of the life, but the ant will demonstrate something that we ourselves are incapable of demonstrating at times. 1:00 You take a grain of sugar, and this I have done, and took a handfull of sand and pour it on the floor and I took the sugar and covered it, and I waited for the first ant came along and watched. For a whole hour that ant bored into that sand and when he left and took off, you know what he was carrying? The grain of sugar not the grain of sand. This is an unusual condition for the ant, the same thing applies to us, we don’t know if we should give it to a human being, “I put this grain of sugar inside this handful of sand, I want you to find the sugar.” With your eyes, fingers, and nose you will not know which one of those grains is sugar, you would have to taste each grain with your tongue to tell which is sugar and the ant will come along and pick up the sugar and go his way. But we have another capacity, the ant couldn’t make a pair of shoes, you see? That’s another outlet for this Creative Intelligence, this Creative Power, birds fly, human beings don’t fly but they have the capacity to make a machinery to fly through the air so each one of us in our own capacity, our own wattage, is uniquely designed by the Creative Intelligence to perceive the source and meditation helps us to enhance this capacity, this wattage, it enhances us to be creative in an outward nature. Not sitting like a lump in the Himalayas, this is not meditation, the first man who saw that thought that it was meditation, “Oh a bunch of lazy people.” Real meditation, when you see we sit down, what we’ve been doing for a few moments, it’s the process, it’s the growing of the plant, it’s not the fruit, it’s not eating it. The eating of fruit, the arrival of the fruit is from here to here to here (Adano far away), that’s one motion of the result of meditation. Now picture the same motions with the meditative result, or picture the same motions with the yogi beads made of tranquilizers. Meditation is a gathering of the forces within ourselves to enhance our own internal verbal capacity to release certain qualities of our own nature and each one of us possess qualities. That’s why the Master’s have said, “Go in first,” before you know the capacity, you will know what the qualities are, you don’t know them, you don’t know what you brought with them, you don’t know how you developed them, but they are there, they are dormant until we go in, then these things are added, these things are released. Now if a scientist is puzzled by an equation, or a problem, or some scientific condition, he has to spend hours of pondering to come up with a solution. It is this same pondering that is bordering on the meditative condition. No scientist will automatically tell you he set out to find the result of a particular experiment and worked all the way to completion, whenever he got to completion it was an accident, he wasn’t looking for it. Everything invention was a result of something else that was not in the process or chain of events that lead to it. It was something that made him conscious of something else. When Edison was trying to make the bulb, you know how many inventions came along, the phonograph and many other things, the last thing he did was the filament and even that was an accident. So we may set out to go to the goal which is God realization by way of meditation, but end up experiencing peace, sensitivity or consciousness, strength, and the abundant extrasensory forces that flow to us like miracles without even asking. That’s like interest on top of your money, we don’t ask for those things because what is the benefit of man “to gain the whole world and lose his soul.” The idea is that we are going God-ward, we are going inwards to a state of awareness of the source and how the source functions, that the source would place us by its own intricate way, highly mathematical, at the right time, the right place, for the right experience, Divine Will, “not my will but Thy will be done.” Even He knew to the last moment, “Don’t weep for Me, weep for yourselves, I am following the Divine Will already.” Now He said, “Father remove this cup,” He was meditating too the Garden of Gethsemane, He saw the conflict between what is the forthcoming way of death, or the forthcoming problem, “Father remove this cup,” but then He corrected Himself because that was ego talking and in the meditative revelation or vision, He saw a higher principle, “Not My will but Thy will be done.” If He imposed His will to have the tragedy, or the forthcoming problem, removed by His extrasensory development and blocking it out by escaping from it, sooner or later He would have to face it, it would be an unfulfilled problem, therefore it would incur repetition, doing it all over again, which you call reincarnating, but if you face it, it’s liberation which lead to resurrection, freedom for the first time. You see freedom lies in involvement and not from running, repetition or being faced with the problem again, lies in avoidance, you got to eat your own words. Meditation is this attitude of going in to establish. They key lies in the eyes, eyes are the windows of the soul. Every attempt of science to validate this principle has been proven, it’s already there and you’ll always see pictures of the realized people, their eyes are like this, turned in to the point between the eyebrows, seeing the focus “When thine eyes are single, the body is full of light.” You see a bit halo around their head, but it’s a physical process and can be experienced by moment-to-moment involvement. Any questions? 1:10 Audience: (inaudible). Adano: Samson was a man who had his eyes wide open until he got so involved that he had to be blinded, but before he was blinded he said, “Lord I thank you for restoring my inner sight which I have lost.” So the blind is not blind, the blind sees in a different realm but they sometimes are closer to the inner light than we are with our eyes wide open. You see that inner light becomes obvious to them in there and you find there is a serenity around a blind person all the time. You wonder why because they should be the person most aggravated because of their lack of sight. It’s not so, you watch the expression of the blind, do not have compassion for the blind, the blind has more compassion for you who think you are seeing because they are experiencing something inside. I’ve talked with the blind and they are so close to inner realization more than those who are wide awake and have their sight. They talk of experiencing that occurence inside and they are fortunate to see outside to disturb this realization that is going on inside. Sometimes blindness is the only karma left for man to renounce the environment so he can go inside and on the inner plane, the realized man these two physical eyes are turned down and this one here is opened up. Therefore paintings, oriental art, show you certain things, you’ll always see the painting of the Buddhas or the Saints, these two turned down and the one here seems to be open. That is because they have shut out the association with the five sense realm and centralized with the internal realm. Now some people, their karma or their thought patterns, since they did not want to face certain things about themselves would shut it out and you notice this psychosomatic principle in some people, afraid to face certain things so they shut out by trying and sooner or later they get weak eyes and they appear to aparent blindness and all type of eye problems and all type of psychiatric problems. Eyes tell us a great deal, they may experience something inside here and because they are trying to express themselves and their very nature is so sensitive and they are not able to communicate and be accepted by others, then they shut it out. They rather shut out the world to maintain what little sanity they think is in there and the world is cruel too and children are cruel. You wanna see why? What is play to a child is death to frogs. There’s a stone, there’s a frog, here’s a five year old kid, “let’s see if you can hit him on the head.” Now one child sees another one and he doesn’t walk past without pushing him. These are actions and one don’t want to be with the group and shuts himself out, he plays and gets into a catatonic state because he’s involved and sometimes there are certain things he sees in himself and he wants to tell it to his parents, the parents think there’s something wrong and then he shuts out the parents sooner or later he becomes the apparent retardedness. It’s happening inside, get him in the presence of someone who is working on the inner plane and that’s why they send them to the ashrams or the Guru’s or spiritual teachers or disciples and we try to pull them out now, not changing their mechanism in no form but allowing them to meditate, to get stronger from inside, to face, not to run away, to face the problems with the inner strength for the first time and then come out and then the parent will see for himself or herself, did the child benefit? We can also approach it by psychological methods, by different techniques of diverting his attention, but that can be very costly. The only cheap method we know of is meditation and children are more adept at meditation than grownups. That’s why Jesus said, “Suffer not the little children that come unto Me, except you be like one of them, thou canst not enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.” They are closer to this inner light, they are not cluttered up with what we call “language symbols” yet. Therefore the free flow of the mind is working through them and meditation becomes easier for them, but because they are not understood and because we don’t understand it ourselves, this is a pretty new worldwide research into ourselves. At one time it was an exclusive group hiding away in the Himalayas and now He’s making it available to everybody with the Beatles and all type of paraphernalia to make us aware that we must look into ourselves. So we are going to appreciate the conditions more of what is happening and in the blind you will see now what a blind man can do. Now Helen Keller was a good example, yet if you ever met Helen Keller’s teacher you wouldn’t like to be around her. Did you ever see the picture about Helen Keller, the teacher appeared to be the most cruel individual yet she was the most important teacher to Helen Keller. If the blind has shut himself out for certain reasons and those reasons are like walls, they are like crusts, they are like barnacles on the inner sensitivity because this inner sensitivity is so fragile. It is the only anchorage, the only security that the inner person has to hold on to for their sanity. They don’t want to give up, they don’t want to come out, they are like the unhatched chicken, he’s born inside the shell but he don’t want to come out, the light is too strong for him, but he needs this outward pressure to force him out, but it must be done with love and yet disciple and yet with frankness and honesty and candor. So then he’ll see that the person who is prompting him to come out has his welfare at heart so the blind now moves from the blind state. Now physiologically if he can’t see, that’s a different story, but if this is a psychological blindness and we’ve had an instance like that in an American composer, his name was WC Handy, the man who wrote the St. Louis Blues. He lost his sight because he playing blues and his father was a minister and his father wanted him to be a good son to follow the church and become a minister and play church music, but when he got blind where did go, in his father’s church to sing hymns and compose When the Saints Come Marching In and all these different things, but he got his sight back and when he got his sight back you know what he told his dad? “I’m laying up treasures in heaven but how about some on Earth right now?” It isn’t that we should not try to utilize what we’ve got, he got his sight back, but that was psychological. It became psychological when his father did not want him to play this music and wanted him to be a church boy and play church music so he built up such internal resentment to the harping of his father’s words and that tone of voice of a parent can get to the point to be very hypnotic. When you listen to your voice scolding or discipline in the child, you are not listening to yourself, you have in your mind to tell them what you want to tell them, he has to listen or she gotta listen to you. He should walk around with a tape recorder and hear “mommy this is what you said to me.” After a year or two you wouldn’t want to talk that way, even if you’re doing the best discipline possible, the tone of voice is disturbing the mechanism of the child. Therefore meditation helps us to discover these things about ourselves and the child or the person is blind can come back out and they will tell you many things about themselves inside of this inner life that they see. 1:20 Audience: Adano last night after almost everyone left, I didn’t hear what you were discussing but it was very interesting about getting rid of the ego, you were discussing the difference between psychic development and spiritual development. I wonder if you care to elaborate on any of those (inaudible). Adano: Yes, alright, we have a natural tendency to want to help our fellow man in his distress. Why this is so is because we are our brother’s keeper but we don’t know how to apply it correctly yet, even in Cain and Abel, “Am I my brother’s keeper?” is what we are. In this sense we share life from within as a common bond so when a person is ill, no matter if it’s your own child or someone else, the illness diminishes you in some way because the vital power is not functioning correctly and in your children is an extension of you no matter if they are cut from the umbilical cord from you, they feel, and other human beings are an extension of you too by consciousness and by life principle. So we feel a sympathetic bond for others, we feel a compassionate bond for others, we want to heal them, we want to do our best to get them well. The Masters have said, “Not until the time comes from within should we heal or do anything for them.” That’s why an example of this was when His mother, Jesus’ mother, came to Him at the very time He came down from the mountain of meditating and He was at a wedding, she came to Him and says “Son, there is no wine” and He said, “Woman, what is that to Me? My time has not yet come.” Well this sound kind of alien to a Jewish home because I’ve been around them and I know the humor and the way they talk to each other, this is highly unfamiliar with a mother and a son talking, it couldn’t be that way, they don’t have the feeling or relationship. What would be more familiar is if her son was named Abe, “Abe my boy, we got no more wine and this is your cousin and the wedding is going on and we got to make merry.” This would be more familiar, this would be more of the nature of a mother to a son. “Momma, momma, that’s not my responsibility.” This would be the normal reaction and I’ve seen in among non-Jew as well as Jew, but this is happening in the Jewish frame of reference so He’s never going to say “woman,” whack, whack across your mouth, “that’s my son the doctor,” “that’s my son the prophet,” they’re proud of it. He’s never going to say “woman” that’s ridiculous, but the point is the endearment between the two by saying “My time has not yet come” is to point out that although she knew He had this ability, all these qualities, and He Himself knew, He did not want to get His ego in the way, you see? We can apply this principle of healing or doing these psychic energies that are a result of meditation, we can apply them in an egotistical way or we can apply it by Divine Will. If we apply it in an egotistical way then we haven’t developed a high level or morality yet, we haven’t reached the state of realization yet, but if we are applying it in Divine Will’s way, we have achieved the realization and the Divine Will works through us and tells us from inside “(inaudible) go ahead” and before you even say “you gonna be healed” the internal self says “I’m healing him already so don’t say nothing.” So “I am My Father are one, I don’t do anything, the Father doeth everything.” This is a confirmation of the fact that “My time has not yet come.” He couldn’t go out and teach this knowledge or make confirmation of a knowledge when the thing was not flowing, He didn’t get the green light to go ahead, you see He was standing at the red light of consciousness waiting with all the forces behind Him, the whole knapsack like Santa Claus, waiting for the red light to come down the chimney. It’s a subtle form of behavior that is very important in trying to help and how to get this thing to go. Now when he taught His disciples and told the world that they can heal or do these things, He made a point of saying, “If you have faith in Me like a grain of mustard seed, you say unto the mountain move and it’ll move.” Most of us try and they say they have faith in God and nothing happens and then they blame God for they don’t get no results but if we understood what the Master Jesus would imply in this faith, He’s not implying a faith in God, He’s implying a faith in ourselves to shift the ego out of the way to allow God. You must have a faith in yourself to get your ego out of the way to allow God to move the mountain for you. Do we move our ego out of the way and let God work for us? Or do we block God and still let our ego stay in the way? “I want this man to be well Lord. I have faith in You he’s gonna be well” but in the first place your ego is so puffed up demanding God to make the man well, God is not making the man well, but if you have the faith to move your ego out of the way and see the Divine Will flow, the man is well. Now a typical example of this getting the ego out of the way, there are many examples in the life of Jesus healing and many other great Saints in the world. Let’s take one that we’re familiar with because we’ve read it so often already, it’s the centurion. He was a Roman and Jesus was of Jewish descent. He left his home because his son was ill with all humility now to humble himself as a conquering nation over another nation that was subjugated to go and ask, these are already the conditions set in motion. You gotta realize what is happening, a man from a high station is like Mr. Johnson coming to ask somebody in Japan to do something for his family, you follow me, it’s the relationship. So he’s coming down from a high level of authority to another level of behavior. When he met the Man, the Master Jesus, if he had any belief that this was a real master that could do something or because his servants told him that this Man did do certain things or he saw some of his servants who were supposed to be ill were well now and he had some conviction that this was his only treasure, his only son, his only heir to all his wealth, he might as well ask, the doctors are not doing anything for him. So he’s humbling himself, he’s going, but he’s ashamed for two reasons: One, he’s a conquering hero and he’s a man of high station. Two, he is not of the same nationality and the same customs and traditions, therefore he would not want that Man into his house, that Man looked down or there would be conflict. 1:30 So these are things he’s fighting with himself, so he comes to the Man and says, “My son is ill, would you come? No, would you just say the word.” He corrects himself. ***

Adano68_11Vol2_OdorofDivinityRRNW6 - 38:00 …Because the object of the meditation is to see this electrical nature of ourcells (ourselves). Now there is no loss, the gentlemen think when you go inside you lose everything, it kind of ridiculous, what you gonna lose? If God already made you this way, what are you going to lose? He made you as a physical form, He made you in electrical form, and He made you as an ideational form, all you’re gonna see is shirt, undershirt, and underpants. You discard it, you’re back where you are in your birthday suit. So when you break through the three levels of your make-up: physical level, electrical level or astral level, idea level, you see yourself as pure life. The odor of divinity, story of Adano in the desert meditating and smelling the love of God, giving love to get love, surrender is being grateful that we are capable of loving God, realization is maturity of love, Masters do everything from devotion which is effort, there is a chemical responsible for love = magnesium / potassium, the human body is the only mechanism that has been chosen for divinity to work with, we cannot escape the constant craving to create because it is part of the creative structure, man was created to realize divinity within himself as a creative force, spiritual explanation of 2001 Space Odyssey movie (the astronaut, monolith, embryo, etc.), survival patterns, how humans have coped with adapting / survival, story about Adano learning to drive a stick shift and having to pick somebody up, he called on Henry Ford to guide him to drive it, he stalled it 20 times before the first stop sign and never got out of first gear, then he gets into second gear, but doesn’t know how to stop, “survival is the thing” so he stops it before somebody hits him, adaptability is survivability, meditation is not a natural thing and it is at the same time, it is not natural for the individual to want peace because he is geared to action, he has to bridge / make the break for meditation to become a natural thing, you have to experience the peace, the challenges in the spiritual life happen when you are repeatedly tested, these challenges produce the strength, respect for the Masters due to the human form making the accomplishment, this is Adano’s realization / his respect, he worships the principle that has come down, he welcomes all the Masters, Adano story about not going to see Yogananda, he realized that time and space would not separate a Master and student needing initiation, until you decide to meditation, the Masters will not come, it is like a TV set on a certain channel, you are interested in the programming coming through the set, not the transmitting station, you are interested in the realization, if we cannot hold on to the eternal principle - the form helps us, strength from form (which will let you down) vs. strength from our own divinity / consciousness, the formless is the end of the road, but you have to go through the interplay of form to get there, the Masters teachings are not written down in a book, it is the interplay between your consciousness and their consciousness, story of Paul going out of the form and realizing the Christ self, but Adano thinks that John’s writings are superior to Paul’s because John’s is personal experience, it is what occurs in everyone’s self, it is abstract / impersonal, he revealed the seven chakras and their divinity within us, he also made us conscious that the attitude of a Master’s life is the key, Master Jesus showed the attitude necessary to go inside, the beatitudes, spiritual realization is not a “holier than thou” attitude, it is the most natural / simple / joyous attitude, meditation is trying to establish inner strength / inner peace in us, the closer you live to the naturalness of yourself / nature will bring it into manifestation, Sri Yukteswar said the deeper the realization of the man / the greater is his influence on creation, explanation of the “pure in heart” physiologically (the decarbonization of blood), must have love or all the techniques will not work, we can go into consciousness and by the love itself / pull the love out, there are two things that tell you if the love of God is flowing through a person: a perfume in the room and a flash of light.

Adano68_11Vol4_GuruDiscipleRelationshipRRNW6 - That is what you call the total surrender to the Divine Will of the Guru. Never questioning Him for no moment.
